criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose lateral myocardial infarction, which of the following diagnostic criterion should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads
Presence of diffuse ST-segment elevation in all leads
Presence of ST-segment elevation in lead aVR
Presence of ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment elevation in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST elevation in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have lateral myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose lateral myocardial infarction?
options: Presence of inverted T waves in lateral leads
Presence of a slurred S wave in lateral leads
Presence of a broad monophasic R wave in lateral leads
Presence of pathological Q waves in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of pathological Q waves in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show pathological Q waves in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6? Note that a pathological Q wave in these leads is defined as (1) a Q wave with a duration greater than 30 milliseconds and an amplitude greater than one fifth of the following R amplitude, or (2) a deep S wave with only a small R wave preceding it.

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show the pathological Q waves? Select all possible leads from the options below.

Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with pathological Q wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have lateral myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
